summary: Excellent soundboard recording of a standout ’76 show with a smoking St. Stephen, Dancing>Wheel, Sugar Begonias and Candyman solo, despite weak Stella Blue and an odd Johnny B. Goode encore.
review: The tape sounds pristine – a crystal‑clear soundboard capture that even benefits from superb remastering. Musically the concert shines with a fiery St. Stephen opening the second set, the mesmerizing Dancing>Wheel sequence, a brilliant Sugar Begonias and a sublime Candyman solo, while the first set stays mellow yet solid. The only real blemishes are a sluggish Stella Blue and a controversial Johnny B. Goode encore.

ai_rating.rationale: Consistently high reviewer scores (mostly 5‑star), strong praise for both performance and audio quality, with only minor song‑specific criticisms.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp, clear mix; guitar and vocals come through distinctly; praised remastering; no reported dropouts or noise.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set described as mellow but solid; second set launches with an incredible St. Stephen, dips briefly before soaring again with Dancing>Wheel and a superb Sugar Begonias, delivering an overall high‑energy performance despite a weak Stella Blue.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Guitar comes through loud, clear and with great tone.
